pages.raw_text:
Great Northern will reduce run
ming time to the coast two hours
Through a new schedule to be
adopted by the Great Northern on
Sunday a saving of II hours will
be effected by passengers from
British Columbia who pass through
Spokane en- route to Vancouver,
Victoria and points ON the British
Columbia coast.


The running time Of train No. 1
between this city and Seattle will
be cut down two hours. arriving
here at a. m. and at Seattle at
1880 p. m, thus enabling it to make
connection at Everett with the
train for Vancouver, B. c., arriving
in the latter city at 10 p. m. No.
3, westbound, will arrive in this
city at 5:45 p. m. instead of 6:2O
p. M, effecting a saving of two
hours under the present schedule.


A new train will be operated be-
tween Spokane and Leavenworth,
leaving this city at p. m. and re-
turning at IL p. M, making all To-
cal stops both ways and carrying
a buffet sleeper. There will be no
change in the running time of train
No. 2, eastbound, which arrives at
9:15 a. m, but train No. will
arrive in Spokane at 1925 p. M, one
hour earlier.


The new Boomers Ferry local
started last week will be discon-
tinued this Sunday. The S. F. & N.
train, southbound, will hereafter
arrive at $50 p. M, five minutes
| earlier
